briefdescription.para: Number of file descriptors which can be added zvfs_fd_set.

detaileddescription.para: Note that these codes are internal Zephyr numbers, for internal Zephyr operations (and subject to change without notice, not part of "stable ABI"). These are however expected to co-exist with "well-known" POSIX/Linux ioctl numbers, and not clash with them.

This function allows to reserve a space for file descriptor entry in the underlying table, and thus allows caller to fail fast if no free descriptor is available. If this function succeeds, zvfs_finalize_fd() or zvfs_free_fd() must be called mandatorily.

simplesect.para: Allocated file descriptor, or -1 in case of error (errno is set)

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, void *obj, const struct fd_op_vtable *vtable, uint32_t mode)
memberdef.name: zvfs_finalize_typed_fd

briefdescription.para: Finalize creation of file descriptor, with type.

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called exactly once after zvfs_reserve_fd(), and should not be called in any other case.
The difference between this function and zvfs_finalize_fd is that the latter does not relay type information of the created file descriptor.
Values permitted for mode are one of ZVFS_MODE_...

parameterdescription.para: File descriptor previously returned by zvfs_reserve_fd()
pointer to I/O operation implementations for the object

detaileddescription.para: This function should be called exactly once after zvfs_reserve_fd(), and should not be called in any other case.

parameterdescription.para: File descriptor previously returned by zvfs_reserve_fd()
pointer to I/O operation implementations for the object

briefdescription.para: Allocate file descriptor for underlying I/O object.

detaileddescription.para: This function combines operations of zvfs_reserve_fd() and zvfs_finalize_fd() in one step, and provided for convenience.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to I/O operation implementations for the object

simplesect.para: Allocated file descriptor, or -1 in case of error (errno is set)

detaileddescription.para: This function may be called once after zvfs_reserve_fd(), and should not be called in any other case.

parameterdescription.para: File descriptor previously returned by zvfs_reserve_fd()

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const struct fd_op_vtable *vtable, int err)
memberdef.name: zvfs_get_fd_obj

briefdescription.para: Get underlying object pointer from file descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This function is useful for functions other than read/write/ioctl to look up underlying I/O object by fd, optionally checking its type (using vtable reference). If fd refers to invalid entry, NULL will be returned with errno set to EBADF. If fd is valid, but vtable param is not NULL and doesn't match object's vtable, NULL is returned and errno set to err param.

parameterdescription.para: File descriptor previously returned by zvfs_reserve_fd()
errno value to set if object vtable doesn't match

simplesect.para: Object pointer or NULL, with errno set

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const struct fd_op_vtable **vtable, struct k_mutex **lock)
memberdef.name: zvfs_get_fd_obj_and_vtable

briefdescription.para: Get underlying object pointer and vtable pointer from file descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: File descriptor previously returned by zvfs_reserve_fd()
A pointer to a pointer variable to store the vtable

An optional pointer to a pointer variable to store the mutex preventing concurrent descriptor access. The lock is not taken, it is just returned for the caller to use if necessary. Pass NULL if the lock is not needed by the caller.

simplesect.para: Object pointer or NULL, with errno set

memberdef.argsstring: (void *obj, const struct fd_op_vtable *vtable, struct k_mutex **lock, struct k_condvar **cond)
memberdef.name: zvfs_get_obj_lock_and_cond

briefdescription.para: Get the mutex and condition variable associated with the given object and vtable.

parameterdescription.para: Object previously returned by a call to e.g. zvfs_get_fd_obj.
A pointer the vtable associated with obj.

An optional pointer to a pointer variable to store the mutex preventing concurrent descriptor access. The lock is not taken, it is just returned for the caller to use if necessary. Pass NULL if the lock is not needed by the caller.

An optional pointer to a pointer variable to store the condition variable to notify waiting threads in the case of concurrent descriptor access. Pass NULL if the condition variable is not needed by the caller.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct fd_op_vtable *vtable, void *obj, unsigned long request,...)
memberdef.name: zvfs_fdtable_call_ioctl

briefdescription.para: Call ioctl vmethod on an object using varargs.

detaileddescription.para: We need this helper function because ioctl vmethod is declared to take va_list and the only portable way to construct va_list is from function's ... parameters.

memberdef.argsstring: (int nfds, struct zvfs_fd_set *ZRESTRICT readfds, struct zvfs_fd_set *ZRESTRICT writefds, struct zvfs_fd_set *ZRESTRICT errorfds, const struct timespec *ZRESTRICT timeout, const void *ZRESTRICT sigmask)
memberdef.name: zvfs_select

briefdescription.para: Open a file with a given name.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to structure with appropriate i/o functions

simplesect.para: Index of newly created file descriptor on success, -1 on error with errno set.
0 on success, -1 with errno set on error.
0 on success, -1 with errno set on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, void *buf, size_t sz, const size_t *from_offset)
memberdef.name: zvfs_read

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to variable specifying starting position; current offset will be used if NULL

simplesect.para: Number of bytes read successfully, or -1 with errno set on failure

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const void *buf, size_t sz, const size_t *from_offset)
memberdef.name: zvfs_write

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to variable specifying starting position; current offset will be used if NULL

simplesect.para: Number of bytes written successfully, or -1 with errno set on failure

parameterdescription.para: Path or name of file to be removed.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 with errno set on error.

briefdescription.para: Rename file or directory, moving it if necessary.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 with errno set on error.
